# DataInfo

> DataInfo is a statistical claim validator. Users submit a claim and the tool checks it against official institutional databases, returning a sourced verdict.

DataInfo is a free web application that verifies statistical and factual claims using data from whitelisted official sources only — no general web crawl. The tool is designed for researchers, students, journalists, and analysts who need to pressure-test a number or claim before citing it.

## How it works

Users type a statistical claim (e.g. "67% of Americans are overweight") and choose a mode:

- **Official Sources mode**: Queries CDC, WHO, World Bank, PubMed, BLS, Census Bureau and other institutional APIs. The language model receives only that fetched data — no web snippets, no hallucinated citations.
- **Web Search mode**: Uses Gemini grounding to search the open web. Sources may include unofficial content.

The tool returns a structured verdict: Verified, Partially Verified, Unverified/False, or No Official Data — along with the metric definition used, alternative definitions, a reasoning trail, and cited sources linking back to the originating agency.

## Data sources

- CDC NHANES, CDC Wonder
- WHO Global Health Observatory
- World Bank Open Data
- PubMed / NCBI
- U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS)
- U.S. Census Bureau
- OECD Stats
- NOAA / climate databases

## Use cases

- Fact-checking statistical claims in journalism, research, and debate
- Verifying figures before citing them in academic work
- Exploring what official data actually says on a topic

## What it is not

DataInfo does not crawl conspiracy sites, blogs, or unverified sources in Official Sources mode. It is not a general search engine.

## Links

- App: https://datainfo.netlify.app
- Backend API: https://datainfo-production.up.railway.app
